title: Molten Italian Briny Calzone
description: A savory, cheesy, and slightly salty solo-dinner featuring a crispy crust, gooey mozzarella, and sashimi-cut peppered mortadella, all infused with the subtle flavor of peppercorns. Perfect for a quick and satisfying dinner on a weeknight.

ingredients.name: Mozzarella Cheese
ingredients.notes: Whole-milk or low-moisture, whichever you prefer

ingredients.name: Mortadella
ingredients.notes: Sashimi-cut, preferably from a deli or high-quality butcher

ingredients.name: Black Peppercorns
ingredients.notes: Freshly cracked, or use whole peppercorns for added flavor and texture

ingredients.name: Sea Salt
ingredients.notes: Flaky or kosher, whichever you have on hand

instructions: Preheat your oven to 450°F (230°C) with a pizza stone or baking sheet inside, if you have one.
Roll out the pizza dough to a thickness of about 1/4 inch (6 mm) and transfer to a lightly floured surface.
In a small bowl, mix together the mozzarella cheese, mortadella, black peppercorns, sea salt, and garlic.
Spread the mozzarella mixture evenly over one half of the dough, leaving a 1-inch (2.5 cm) border around the edges.
Drizzle the olive oil over the mozzarella mixture, then sprinkle with a pinch of black pepper for added flavor.
Fold the other half of the dough over the filling to form a half-circle or triangle, pressing the edges together to seal the calzone.
Place the calzone on a piece of parchment paper or a lightly floured baking sheet, if you don't have parchment paper.
Brush the top of the calzone with a little olive oil to prevent it from becoming too dark during baking.
Bake the calzone in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes, or until it's golden brown and the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the calzone from the oven and let it cool for a minute or two before slicing and serving.
Garnish with chopped fresh basil for added flavor and texture.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Reheat the calzone in the oven at 350°F (180°C) for 5-7 minutes, or until warmed through and crispy.
